title: Gaurishvara Temple, Yelandur
tagline: Gaurishvara Temple, Yelandur — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: The Gaurishvara Temple (also spelled Gaurishwara or Gaurisvara) is located in the town of Yelandur, Chamarajanagar district of Karnataka state, India.
overview.description: The Gaurishvara Temple (also spelled Gaurishwara or Gaurisvara) is located in the town of Yelandur, Chamarajanagar district of Karnataka state, India. The temple was constructed by a local chief Singedepa Devabhupala of the Hadinadu chiefdom, a feudatory of the 16th century Vijayanagara Empire.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Gaurishvara Temple, Yelandur?
faq.a: The best time to visit Gaurishvara Temple, Yelandur is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Gaurishvara Temple, Yelandur?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mysuru Airport (45 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (52 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Gaurishvara Temple, Yelandur?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Gaurishvara Temple, Yelandur at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Gaurishvara Temple, Yelandur?
faq.a: Stays in Gaurishvara Temple, Yelandur start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Gaurishvara Temple, Yelandur?
faq.a: The top attractions are Yeragamballi, Ambale, Chamarajanagar, Mamballi, Kesthur, Yelandur.
